comments.body:
I traced the linked run. It did not stop because of the round limit or a failed `fix-pr`; it stopped because the **agent-mode orchestrator planner could not complete its GitHub reads**.

What happened:

- `fix-pr` completed and handed back to the orchestrator as intended: [`agent-fix-pr.yml#L277-L297`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-fix-pr.yml#L277-L297).
- The orchestrator ran in `agent` mode, round `4/10`, so it invoked the planner step with `permission_mode: approve-reads`: [`agent-orchestrator.yml#L120-L157`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml#L120-L157).
- The planner tried to inspect fresh state with:
  - `gh pr view 45 ...`
  - `gh issue view 33 ...`
- `acpx` treated those as commands needing approval, but this is a non-interactive GitHub Actions run, so the permission prompt was aborted. The planner exited without valid JSON.
- The workflow intentionally continues after planner failure, then `orchestrate-handoff` sees no valid planner decision and stops here: [`handoff.ts#L296-L300`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L296-L300).

Relevant run: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/actions/runs/25140345554

There is also a secondary access warning in the same run: thread-state pre-run push failed with `Permission to self-evolving/repo.git denied to github-actions[bot]`. That came from the orchestrator workflow checking out with `github.token` while the workflow only grants `contents: read`: [`agent-orchestrator.yml#L62-L80`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml#L62-L80). It did **not** cause this stop because the session policy was `resume-best-effort`, but it means planner session continuity was not persisted.

Proposed fixes:

1. **Best fix:** prefetch required PR/issue/review/comment context deterministically before the planner runs, using workflow/runtime code, then pass that snapshot into the planner prompt. The planner should not need to call `gh` at all for normal orchestration decisions.

2. **Quick fix:** change the orchestrator planner permission mode from `approve-reads` to `approve-all`, matching other context-gathering routes documented in [`request-lifecycle.md#L39-L49`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/architecture/request-lifecycle.md#L39-L49). This is simple, but it broadens tool access for a planner that should ideally stay read-only.

3. **Middle ground:** keep `approve-reads`, but teach the runtime/acpx policy to auto-allow known read-only commands for the orchestrator planner, e.g. `gh pr view`, `gh issue view`, and possibly `gh api graphql` when used for reads.

4. **Fix the secondary continuity warning:** either checkout with the resolved app token, or grant the orchestrator enough `contents` permission for thread-state ref pushes. Since this was non-blocking, I would treat it as a separate follow-up unless we want planner resumability to be reliable immediately.

My recommendation: implement option 1 for robustness, with option 3 as a small compatibility layer. That keeps the planner constrained, avoids non-interactive approval prompts, and makes orchestration decisions reproducible from an explicit context bundle.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `implement` (round 2/10).

manual orchestrate start on issue; dispatching implement

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777521386638 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNDY1MjE0ODQ6b3JjaGVzdHJhdGU6NDg6aW1wbGVtZW50OjQ4OjI -->

**Sepo implementation finished**

- Branch: `agent/implement-issue-48/codex-25146535432`
- Pull request: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/49

Changed the agent-mode orchestrator planner to run with `approve-all` permissions so it can gather GitHub/repository context in non-interactive workflow runs. Updated the request lifecycle and orchestrator docs, added a workflow contract assertion, and verified with `npm run build` plus `node --test dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`.

title: orchestrator access issue
